success_surface: Sheet-Level Text Replacement with Formatting Preservation
effective_behaviors: Perform structural exploration before modifying (check sheets, dimensions, formulas, data types)
Use openpyxl for cell-value string transformations requiring style/formula preservation
Iterate comprehensively across full worksheet dimensions (all rows × all columns) for sheet-level operations
Apply conditional modifications only to cells meeting specific criteria (e.g., contain target substring)
Validate results by reloading and inspecting output file before completing task
Ensure output directories exist before writing files
Close workbook handles explicitly after save operations
evidence: Agent first inspected workbook structure (sheets count, max_row, max_column, formulas found) before executing replacement
Solution used openpyxl.load_workbook() and direct cell.value assignment instead of pandas
Nested loop processed range(1, max_row+1) × range(1, max_column+1) covering entire worksheet
Conditional check 'if "\"' in original_value' prevented unnecessary writes to non-matching cells
Re-loaded output file and printed verification log confirming quote removal succeeded

memory_items.title: Structural Exploration Before Modification
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure (sheets, dimensions, formulas, data types) before applying sheet-level transformations
memory_items.generalization: Reduce error risk by understanding data scope and identifying potential edge cases like formulas or merged cells before executing bulk operations

memory_items.title: Full Dimension Iteration for Sheet-Wide Changes
memory_items.lesson: When instructed to modify an entire worksheet, iterate across maximum rows AND maximum columns, not just visible or expected ranges
memory_items.generalization: Prevent partial updates by using ws.max_row and ws.max_column to dynamically determine actual worksheet boundaries

memory_items.title: Content-Aware Conditional Modification
memory_items.lesson: Only apply transformations to cells that actually match the criteria (e.g., contain target substring, specific data type)
memory_items.generalization: Avoid unintended side effects by checking cell conditions before modifying values, especially for find-and-replace operations

memory_items.title: Post-Operation Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Reload the saved output file and verify critical changes before considering the task complete
memory_items.generalization: Catch save/write failures early by validating against expected outcomes in a fresh workbook instance

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### 1. Exploration First, Modify Later
Before executing any sheet-wide transformation:
- Load the workbook and identify target worksheets
- Query ws.max_row and ws.max_column to determine actual dimensions
- Check for formulas (cells starting with '=') that may be affected
- Detect data types present (strings, numbers, dates)

This prevents partial updates and identifies edge cases early.

### 2. Full-Scope Iteration Pattern
For sheet-level operations, always use nested iteration:
```python
for row_idx in range(1, ws.max_row + 1):
    for col_idx in range(1, ws.max_column + 1):
        # Process cell
```
Do not assume fixed ranges; rely on dynamic dimension detection.

### 3. Content-Aware Transformations
Apply modifications conditionally:
- Check cell.value is not None
- Verify the value matches operation criteria (e.g., contains substring)
- Convert to appropriate type before comparison

This minimizes unnecessary writes and preserves non-targeted data.

### 4. Verification Before Completion
After saving:
- Reload the output file as a separate Workbook object
- Inspect sample cells to confirm expected changes
- Print summary logs for debugging purposes
- Close both input and output workbooks properly
